content.content:
1	# things-sync
2	
3	Python wrapper for Things 3. Two layers, by what each is best at:
4	
5	- **`Things`** — façade. Writes go through AppleScript (`osascript`),
6	  reads through the SQLite layer below. Synchronous against Things'
7	  local store: a write returns once Things has committed it, so a
8	  follow-up read sees it immediately.
9	- **`ThingsDB`** — read-only SQLite at disk speed, reading Things' own
10	  database. Same dataclasses as the façade returns from writes.
11	
12	Things 3 must be installed and **running** on the local Mac. There is
13	no networked / cloud-direct write path — every write is an AS call
14	against the local app. Reads work whether the app is foreground or
15	background, but the app process must be alive for writes.
16	
17	## Install
18	
19	```bash
20	uv sync --extra dev
21	```
22	
23	(Or, if vendoring into another project, `uv add` from a local path.)
24	
25	Zero non-stdlib runtime dependencies.
26	
27	## Quickstart
28	
29	```python
30	from things_sync import Things
31	
32	t = Things()
33	
34	project = t.create_project("Plan trip", deadline="2026-05-01")
35	todo = t.create_todo(
36	    "Book flights",
37	    notes="aisle seat please",
38	    deadline="2026-04-29",
39	    tags=["travel"],
40	    project=project.id,
41	)
42	
43	t.update_todo(todo.id, notes="window seat actually")
44	t.complete(todo.id)
45	
46	t.delete(todo.id)        # → Trash (recoverable)
47	t.empty_trash()          # purge
48	```
49	
50	## Entity & operation matrix
51	
52	| Entity   | Create | Read | Edit fields | Status | Trash / purge |
53	|----------|--------|------|-------------|--------|---------------|
54	| Todo     | AS     | DB   | name, notes, due_date, when, tags, project, area, contact | open / completed / canceled | `delete` + `empty_trash` |
55	| Project  | AS     | DB   | name, notes, due_date, tags, area | open / completed / canceled | `delete` + `empty_trash` |
56	| Area     | AS     | DB   | name, tags, collapsed | n/a | `delete` |
57	| Tag      | AS     | DB   | name, shortcut, parent | n/a | `delete` |
58	| Contact  | AS     | DB   | not exposed | n/a | not exposed |
59	| Heading  | Shortcut | DB | not exposed | n/a | Shortcut |
60	
61	### Editing semantics
62	
63	`update_todo` / `update_project` / `update_area` / `update_tag` accept
64	keyword arguments only. Pass a value to set it; **omit** the keyword to
65	leave the field alone.
66	
67	```python
68	t.update_todo(todo.id, name="Book trains instead", notes="rebook",
69	              due_date="2026-05-15", tags=["travel", "urgent"])
70	t.update_project(project.id, name="Plan adventure",
71	                 due_date="2026-06-01", area=area.id)
72	t.update_area(area.id, name="Personal", collapsed=True)
73	t.update_tag(tag.id, name="errands", shortcut="e")
74	```
75	
76	For nullable parents on todos (`project=None`, `area=None`,
77	`contact=None`) — passing `None` clears the parent. Clearing
78	`project` or `area` routes the todo back to Inbox (Things' AS
79	move-to-list, which clears project + area + heading at once).
80	
81	### Status moves
82	
83	```python
84	t.complete(todo.id)
85	t.cancel(todo.id)
86	t.reopen(todo.id)
87	```
88	
89	For projects, status goes through `update_project(id, status=…)` with
90	`Status.OPEN` / `Status.COMPLETED` / `Status.CANCELED`.
91	
92	### Schedule / move
93	
94	`when` is the start/scheduled date that decides which built-in list
95	(Today / Upcoming / Anytime / Someday) the todo shows up in.
96	
97	```python
98	from datetime import date
99	
100	t.schedule(todo.id, date.today())            # → Today
101	t.schedule(todo.id, "2026-05-10")            # → Upcoming, then Today on the day
102	t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Anytime")           # clear schedule
103	t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Someday")
104	t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Inbox")             # also clears project/area
105	t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Trash")             # → soft-trash
106	t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Logbook")           # → complete
107	
108	t.move_to_project(todo.id, project.id)
109	t.move_to_area(todo.id, area.id)
110	```
111	
112	`Upcoming` is derived from a future schedule date — use `schedule`,
113	not `move_to_list("Upcoming")` (which raises).
114	
115	### Deletion
116	
117	```python
118	t.delete(todo.id)            # soft, → Trash, recoverable
119	t.empty_trash()              # purges all currently-trashed items
120	t.delete_immediately(todo.id)  # convenience: trash + empty
121	```
122	
123	`delete` works on todos, projects, areas, and tags. (Tags and areas
124	are removed outright; todos and projects move to Trash.)
125	
126	## API surface
127	
128	All methods live on `Things`. Identifiers are Things' own UUIDs
129	(returned on every create / read).
130	
131	**Reads.** `version`, `count_todos`, `count_projects`, `count_areas`,
132	`count_tags`, `lists`, `todos`, `todo`, `todos_in_list`,
133	`todos_in_project`, `todos_in_area`, `todos_with_tag`, `selected_todos`,
134	`projects`, `project`, `headings`, `areas`, `area`, `tags`, `tag`,
135	`contacts`, `exists`.
136	
137	**Create.** `create_todo`, `create_project`, `create_area`,
138	`create_tag`, `create_contact`, `parse_quicksilver`.
139	
140	**Update.** `update_todo`, `update_project`, `update_area`, `update_tag`.
141	
142	**Status.** `complete`, `cancel`, `reopen`.
143	
144	**Move / schedule.** `move_to_list`, `move_to_area`, `move_to_project`,
145	`schedule`.
146	
147	**Delete.** `delete` (→ Trash for todos/projects, outright for areas/
148	tags), `empty_trash`, `delete_immediately`.
149	
150	**UI.** `show`, `edit`, `show_quick_entry`, `launch`, `quit`,
151	`is_running`.
152	
153	**Maintenance.** `log_completed_now`.
154	
155	## Fast reads (`ThingsDB`)
156	
157	AppleScript enumeration is dominated by per-property IPC roundtrips —
158	reading every todo with all fields can take seconds per hundred items.
159	`ThingsDB` skips AppleScript entirely and reads Things' on-disk SQLite
160	file directly. Same dataclasses, same fields, 100–1000× faster:
161	
162	```python
163	from things_sync import ThingsDB, Status
164	
165	db = ThingsDB()                      # autodetects ~/Library/Group Containers/…
166	open_todos = [t for t in db.todos() if t.status == Status.OPEN]
167	projects   = db.projects()
168	headings   = db.headings()
169	areas      = db.areas()
170	tags       = db.tags()
171	```
172	
173	The DB is opened in `mode=ro` and is WAL-mode, so reading while the
174	app is running is safe. Pass `ThingsDB(path=…)` if your install lives
175	in a non-standard container.
176	
177	## Models
178	
179	Frozen dataclasses in `things_sync.models`: `Todo`, `Project`, `Heading`,
180	`Area`, `Tag`, `Contact`, `ListInfo`, plus the `Status` enum
181	(`open`/`completed`/`canceled`) and `StartBucket`
182	(`inbox`/`anytime`/`someday`).
183	
184	## Known limits
185	
186	The AS dictionary doesn't cover everything Things' UI exposes; we
187	keep the wrapper to what AS can actually do reliably:
188	
189	- **Headings: create + delete via Shortcuts; rename UI-only.**
190	  AppleScript has no `heading` class at all — neither `make new
191	  heading` nor `delete (heading id ...)` parse. Both create and
192	  delete route through Shortcuts.app:
193	  `Things.create_heading(project_id, name)` shells out to
194	  `shortcuts run "Things-Sync Add Heading"`, and
195	  `Things.delete_heading(heading_id)` shells out to
196	  `shortcuts run "Things-Sync Delete Heading"`. Both Shortcuts
197	  must be set up once by the user — see the docstrings on the
198	  two methods for the action recipes. Renaming a heading and
199	  reassigning todos under one are still UI-only.
200	- **No programmatic date clearing.** AS rejects
201	  `set due date of t to missing value` for date-typed properties.
202	  `update_todo(id, due_date=None)` raises. Clear due dates from
203	  the Things UI.
204	- **No `lists` collection enumeration.** The AS `lists` accessor is
205	  broken on Things 3.22.11; `Things.lists()` returns built-in names
206	  hardcoded, and `todos_in_list(name)` reads via SQLite.
207	
208	## Tests
209	
210	```bash
211	uv run pytest
212	```
213	
214	Live tests run against the Things 3 instance on this machine. Every
215	created entity is name-prefixed `_ts_test_` and purged in teardown.
216	
217	**Do not run against a Things account holding real data.** Cleanup
218	is best-effort and AS bugs in the test suite can leave residue under
219	the `_ts_test_` prefix.
220	
221	## How it works
222	
223	Source layout:
224	
225	```
226	src/things_sync/
227	  __init__.py     — public exports
228	  models.py       — dataclasses
229	  things.py       — Things class (façade, AS writers)
230	  _db.py          — ThingsDB (read-only SQLite)
231	  _osascript.py   — osascript runner + delimited-output parser
232	  _scripts.py     — AppleScript prelude + per-class serializers
233	```
234	
235	Writes pipe an AppleScript through `osascript -`. The prelude defines
236	field/record separators (ASCII US `\x1f` and RS `\x1e`) and per-class
237	serializers; write bodies build a `tell {Things3}` block, mutate the
238	target entity, and return its serialized record. Reads through
239	`ThingsDB` open Things' SQLite at
240	`~/Library/Group Containers/JLMPQHK86H.com.culturedcode.ThingsMac/`
241	in read-only mode.
242

# things-sync

Python wrapper for Things 3. Two layers, by what each is best at:

- **`Things`** — façade. Writes go through AppleScript (`osascript`),
  reads through the SQLite layer below. Synchronous against Things'
  local store: a write returns once Things has committed it, so a
  follow-up read sees it immediately.
- **`ThingsDB`** — read-only SQLite at disk speed, reading Things' own
  database. Same dataclasses as the façade returns from writes.

Things 3 must be installed and **running** on the local Mac. There is
no networked / cloud-direct write path — every write is an AS call
against the local app. Reads work whether the app is foreground or
background, but the app process must be alive for writes.

## Install

```bash
uv sync --extra dev
```

(Or, if vendoring into another project, `uv add` from a local path.)

Zero non-stdlib runtime dependencies.

## Quickstart

```python
from things_sync import Things

t = Things()

project = t.create_project("Plan trip", deadline="2026-05-01")
todo = t.create_todo(
    "Book flights",
    notes="aisle seat please",
    deadline="2026-04-29",
    tags=["travel"],
    project=project.id,
)

t.update_todo(todo.id, notes="window seat actually")
t.complete(todo.id)

t.delete(todo.id)        # → Trash (recoverable)
t.empty_trash()          # purge
```

## Entity & operation matrix

| Entity   | Create | Read | Edit fields | Status | Trash / purge |
|----------|--------|------|-------------|--------|---------------|
| Todo     | AS     | DB   | name, notes, due_date, when, tags, project, area, contact | open / completed / canceled | `delete` + `empty_trash` |
| Project  | AS     | DB   | name, notes, due_date, tags, area | open / completed / canceled | `delete` + `empty_trash` |
| Area     | AS     | DB   | name, tags, collapsed | n/a | `delete` |
| Tag      | AS     | DB   | name, shortcut, parent | n/a | `delete` |
| Contact  | AS     | DB   | not exposed | n/a | not exposed |
| Heading  | Shortcut | DB | not exposed | n/a | Shortcut |

### Editing semantics

`update_todo` / `update_project` / `update_area` / `update_tag` accept
keyword arguments only. Pass a value to set it; **omit** the keyword to
leave the field alone.

```python
t.update_todo(todo.id, name="Book trains instead", notes="rebook",
              due_date="2026-05-15", tags=["travel", "urgent"])
t.update_project(project.id, name="Plan adventure",
                 due_date="2026-06-01", area=area.id)
t.update_area(area.id, name="Personal", collapsed=True)
t.update_tag(tag.id, name="errands", shortcut="e")
```

For nullable parents on todos (`project=None`, `area=None`,
`contact=None`) — passing `None` clears the parent. Clearing
`project` or `area` routes the todo back to Inbox (Things' AS
move-to-list, which clears project + area + heading at once).

### Status moves

```python
t.complete(todo.id)
t.cancel(todo.id)
t.reopen(todo.id)
```

For projects, status goes through `update_project(id, status=…)` with
`Status.OPEN` / `Status.COMPLETED` / `Status.CANCELED`.

### Schedule / move

`when` is the start/scheduled date that decides which built-in list
(Today / Upcoming / Anytime / Someday) the todo shows up in.

```python
from datetime import date

t.schedule(todo.id, date.today())            # → Today
t.schedule(todo.id, "2026-05-10")            # → Upcoming, then Today on the day
t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Anytime")           # clear schedule
t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Someday")
t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Inbox")             # also clears project/area
t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Trash")             # → soft-trash
t.move_to_list(todo.id, "Logbook")           # → complete

t.move_to_project(todo.id, project.id)
t.move_to_area(todo.id, area.id)
```

`Upcoming` is derived from a future schedule date — use `schedule`,
not `move_to_list("Upcoming")` (which raises).

### Deletion

```python
t.delete(todo.id)            # soft, → Trash, recoverable
t.empty_trash()              # purges all currently-trashed items
t.delete_immediately(todo.id)  # convenience: trash + empty
```

`delete` works on todos, projects, areas, and tags. (Tags and areas
are removed outright; todos and projects move to Trash.)

## API surface

All methods live on `Things`. Identifiers are Things' own UUIDs
(returned on every create / read).

**Reads.** `version`, `count_todos`, `count_projects`, `count_areas`,
`count_tags`, `lists`, `todos`, `todo`, `todos_in_list`,
`todos_in_project`, `todos_in_area`, `todos_with_tag`, `selected_todos`,
`projects`, `project`, `headings`, `areas`, `area`, `tags`, `tag`,
`contacts`, `exists`.

**Create.** `create_todo`, `create_project`, `create_area`,
`create_tag`, `create_contact`, `parse_quicksilver`.

**Update.** `update_todo`, `update_project`, `update_area`, `update_tag`.

**Status.** `complete`, `cancel`, `reopen`.

**Move / schedule.** `move_to_list`, `move_to_area`, `move_to_project`,
`schedule`.

**Delete.** `delete` (→ Trash for todos/projects, outright for areas/
tags), `empty_trash`, `delete_immediately`.

**UI.** `show`, `edit`, `show_quick_entry`, `launch`, `quit`,
`is_running`.

**Maintenance.** `log_completed_now`.

## Fast reads (`ThingsDB`)

AppleScript enumeration is dominated by per-property IPC roundtrips —
reading every todo with all fields can take seconds per hundred items.
`ThingsDB` skips AppleScript entirely and reads Things' on-disk SQLite
file directly. Same dataclasses, same fields, 100–1000× faster:

```python
from things_sync import ThingsDB, Status

db = ThingsDB()                      # autodetects ~/Library/Group Containers/…
open_todos = [t for t in db.todos() if t.status == Status.OPEN]
projects   = db.projects()
headings   = db.headings()
areas      = db.areas()
tags       = db.tags()
```

The DB is opened in `mode=ro` and is WAL-mode, so reading while the
app is running is safe. Pass `ThingsDB(path=…)` if your install lives
in a non-standard container.

## Models

Frozen dataclasses in `things_sync.models`: `Todo`, `Project`, `Heading`,
`Area`, `Tag`, `Contact`, `ListInfo`, plus the `Status` enum
(`open`/`completed`/`canceled`) and `StartBucket`
(`inbox`/`anytime`/`someday`).

## Known limits

The AS dictionary doesn't cover everything Things' UI exposes; we
keep the wrapper to what AS can actually do reliably:

- **Headings: create + delete via Shortcuts; rename UI-only.**
  AppleScript has no `heading` class at all — neither `make new
  heading` nor `delete (heading id ...)` parse. Both create and
  delete route through Shortcuts.app:
  `Things.create_heading(project_id, name)` shells out to
  `shortcuts run "Things-Sync Add Heading"`, and
  `Things.delete_heading(heading_id)` shells out to
  `shortcuts run "Things-Sync Delete Heading"`. Both Shortcuts
  must be set up once by the user — see the docstrings on the
  two methods for the action recipes. Renaming a heading and
  reassigning todos under one are still UI-only.
- **No programmatic date clearing.** AS rejects
  `set due date of t to missing value` for date-typed properties.
  `update_todo(id, due_date=None)` raises. Clear due dates from
  the Things UI.
- **No `lists` collection enumeration.** The AS `lists` accessor is
  broken on Things 3.22.11; `Things.lists()` returns built-in names
  hardcoded, and `todos_in_list(name)` reads via SQLite.

## Tests

```bash
uv run pytest
```

Live tests run against the Things 3 instance on this machine. Every
created entity is name-prefixed `_ts_test_` and purged in teardown.

**Do not run against a Things account holding real data.** Cleanup
is best-effort and AS bugs in the test suite can leave residue under
the `_ts_test_` prefix.

## How it works

Source layout:

```
src/things_sync/
  __init__.py     — public exports
  models.py       — dataclasses
  things.py       — Things class (façade, AS writers)
  _db.py          — ThingsDB (read-only SQLite)
  _osascript.py   — osascript runner + delimited-output parser
  _scripts.py     — AppleScript prelude + per-class serializers
```

Writes pipe an AppleScript through `osascript -`. The prelude defines
field/record separators (ASCII US `\x1f` and RS `\x1e`) and per-class
serializers; write bodies build a `tell {Things3}` block, mutate the
target entity, and return its serialized record. Reads through
`ThingsDB` open Things' SQLite at
`~/Library/Group Containers/JLMPQHK86H.com.culturedcode.ThingsMac/`
in read-only mode.

"""Atomic event-change feed for Timelines.

The cloud sync backend speaks in atomic *changesets*: each carries a list of
per-event ops — upsert (create), partial (edit, only the changed fields), or
delete. :func:`sync.fetch_cloud_state` folds those into final state and discards
the deltas; this module instead exposes the same stream as a list of decoded
per-event changes *since a given counter*.

It is deliberately **stateless and side-effect-free**: it talks to the cloud and
returns data. It does not persist a cursor, write a log, or decide what "since"
means on a first run — those are the caller's policy (e.g. Atlas keeps a cursor
and appends a JSONL log; ``tt`` just communicates).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any

from . import api
from .coredata import CHANGE_DELETE, CHANGE_PARTIAL, CHANGE_UPSERT

# changeType -> action name. Upsert reads as a create; partial as an edit (it
# carries only the fields that changed); delete as a delete.
ACTIONS: dict[int, str] = {
    CHANGE_UPSERT: "create",
    CHANGE_PARTIAL: "edit",
    CHANGE_DELETE: "delete",
}

# The mutable event fields we surface on a change, in tt's standard wire
# vocabulary (the same keys ``Event.to_json_dict`` / the cloud wire use). A
# change carries only the subset that actually changed.
CHANGE_FIELDS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    "name",
    "notes",
    "timelineIdentifier",
    "startDate",
    "endDate",
)


def decode_event_change(cs: dict[str, Any], ec: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Decode one event change into a plain record.

    ``changedFields`` holds only the tracked fields present on the op — for a
    partial edit that's exactly what changed; for a create it's all of them; for
    a delete it's empty. Dates pass through as the server's ISO strings.
    ``timestamp`` is the op's own ``lastModifiedDate`` when present (edits and
    creates stamp it), falling back to the changeset timestamp (deletes carry no
    per-op timestamp).
    """
    return {
        "globalCounter": cs.get("globalCounter"),
        "timestamp": ec.get("lastModifiedDate") or cs.get("timestamp"),
        "action": ACTIONS.get(ec.get("changeType"), "unknown"),
        "uniqueIdentifier": ec.get("uniqueIdentifier"),
        "changedFields": {k: ec[k] for k in CHANGE_FIELDS if k in ec},
    }


def decode_changesets(changesets: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Every event change across the changesets, in globalCounter order."""
    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for cs in sorted(changesets, key=lambda c: c.get("globalCounter") or 0):
        for ec in cs.get("eventChanges") or []:
            if ec.get("uniqueIdentifier"):
                out.append(decode_event_change(cs, ec))
    return out


def current_counter() -> int:
    """The cloud's current globalCounter — what a caller would store to start
    watching from "now" without replaying history."""
    return api.SyncClient().discover_counter()


def changes_since(counter: int) -> tuple[int, list[dict[str, Any]]]:
    """Pull every changeset newer than ``counter`` and decode its event changes.

    Returns ``(new_counter, changes)`` where ``new_counter`` is the highest
    globalCounter pulled (advanced over *all* changesets, including
    non-event ones, so the caller never re-pulls or loops) — or ``counter``
    unchanged if nothing was newer.
    """
    client = api.SyncClient()
    changesets = client.pull_changesets_since(counter)
    changes = decode_changesets(changesets)
    new_counter = max(
        (int(cs.get("globalCounter") or 0) for cs in changesets), default=counter
    )
    return new_counter, changes

1	"""Direct HTTP client for the Glimsoft Timelines cloud sync backend.
2	
3	Replaces the need to open the Timelines app after every CLI write. We talk
4	straight to `https://cloud.timelines.app/sync` using the sync-hash formula
5	reverse-engineered on 2026-04-14 via mitmproxy capture:
6	
7	    empty pull:  sha256(str(counter) + SALT)
8	    write:       sha256(str(first_changeset_id) + str(counter) + SALT)
9	
10	where SALT = "mY-CuSTom_s_A_l_T". The write hash binds the client-generated
11	changeset UUID to the counter; the body bytes are not hashed.
12	
13	Auth is scraped from the Timelines app's own NSURLCache (`Cache.db`), where
14	the most recent /sync request is archived with its Bearer token and the
15	device identifier. No signup / signin needed.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import gzip
21	import hashlib
22	import json
23	import os
24	import plistlib
25	import sqlite3
26	import uuid
27	from dataclasses import dataclass
28	from datetime import datetime, timezone
29	from pathlib import Path
30	from typing import Any
31	from urllib.error import HTTPError, URLError
32	from urllib.request import Request, urlopen
33	
34	
35	SALT = "mY-CuSTom_s_A_l_T"
36	SYNC_URL = "https://cloud.timelines.app/sync"
37	DISCOVER_COUNTER = 999999  # any large value — server returns actual counter
38	
39	# The iOS-on-Mac container UUID differs per install, so glob for it.
40	CACHE_DB_GLOB = "Library/Containers/*/Data/Library/Caches/com.glimsoft.Timelines/Cache.db"
41	
42	
43	def find_cache_db() -> Path:
44	    matches = sorted(Path.home().glob(CACHE_DB_GLOB))
45	    if not matches:
46	        raise FileNotFoundError(
47	            f"No Timelines Cache.db found under ~/{CACHE_DB_GLOB}. "
48	            "Install the Timelines iOS app from the Mac App Store and open it "
49	            "once so it hits /sync."
50	        )
51	    return max(matches, key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime)
52	
53	STORE_PATH = Path.home() / "Library/Group Containers/group.glimsoft.Timelines/TimelinesModel"
54	
55	CORE_DATA_EPOCH = 978307200.0  # seconds between 1970 and 2001
56	
57	
58	@dataclass
59	class SyncAuth:
60	    token: str       # bearer token, without the "Bearer " prefix
61	    device_uuid: str # the deviceIdentifier string
62	
63	
64	# ---------- Auth discovery ----------
65	
66	ENV_TOKEN = "TIMELINES_AUTH_TOKEN"
67	ENV_DEVICE_UUID = "TIMELINES_DEVICE_UUID"
68	
69	# Saved-session file. Mirrors how `ntn`/`td` persist a session under
70	# ~/.config/<tool>/ so you don't re-export env vars every shell. Since there's
71	# no real signin flow, `tt auth` bootstraps this file from the env vars (loaded
72	# by direnv from .envrc) or, as a fallback, from the app's Cache.db.
73	CONFIG_DIR = Path.home() / ".config" / "timelines"
74	SESSION_PATH = CONFIG_DIR / "auth.json"
75	
76	
77	def load_auth_from_env() -> SyncAuth | None:
78	    """Build auth from the TIMELINES_AUTH_TOKEN / TIMELINES_DEVICE_UUID env
79	    vars (e.g. exported by direnv or your shell profile). Returns None if
80	    either is unset."""
81	    token = os.environ.get(ENV_TOKEN)
82	    device_uuid = os.environ.get(ENV_DEVICE_UUID)
83	    if not token or not device_uuid:
84	        return None
85	    return SyncAuth(
86	        token=token.removeprefix("Bearer ").strip(),
87	        device_uuid=device_uuid.strip(),
88	    )
89	
90	
91	def load_auth_from_file(path: Path = SESSION_PATH) -> SyncAuth | None:
92	    """Read a saved session from the on-disk session file. Returns None if the
93	    file is missing, unreadable, or incomplete."""
94	    try:
95	        data = json.loads(path.read_text())
96	    except (FileNotFoundError, json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
97	        return None
98	    token = data.get("token")
99	    device_uuid = data.get("device_uuid")
100	    if not token or not device_uuid:
101	        return None
102	    return SyncAuth(
103	        token=str(token).removeprefix("Bearer ").strip(),
104	        device_uuid=str(device_uuid).strip(),
105	    )
106	
107	
108	def save_auth(auth: SyncAuth, path: Path = SESSION_PATH) -> Path:
109	    """Persist a session to the on-disk session file (mode 0600). Returns the
110	    path written."""
111	    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
112	    path.write_text(
113	        json.dumps(
114	            {"version": 1, "token": auth.token, "device_uuid": auth.device_uuid},
115	            indent=2,
116	        )
117	        + "\n"
118	    )
119	    path.chmod(0o600)
120	    return path
121	
122	
123	def load_auth() -> SyncAuth:
124	    """Resolve sync auth, erroring clearly if unavailable.
125	
126	    Resolution order: the TIMELINES_AUTH_TOKEN + TIMELINES_DEVICE_UUID env vars
127	    (override, e.g. for CI/direnv), then the saved session file. Run `tt auth`
128	    to populate the session file."""
129	    auth = load_auth_from_env() or load_auth_from_file()
130	    if auth is None:
131	        raise RuntimeError(
132	            f"No Timelines auth. Set {ENV_TOKEN} + {ENV_DEVICE_UUID} (e.g. via "
133	            f"direnv), or run `tt auth` to save a session to {SESSION_PATH}."
134	        )
135	    return auth
136	
137	
138	def load_auth_from_cache(cache_db: Path | None = None) -> SyncAuth:
139	    """Pull token + device UUID out of the Timelines app's CFURLCache."""
140	    if cache_db is None:
141	        cache_db = find_cache_db()
142	    if not cache_db.exists():
143	        raise FileNotFoundError(
144	            f"Timelines Cache.db not found at {cache_db}. Has the app ever "
145	            "opened and synced at least once?"
146	        )
147	    conn = sqlite3.connect(str(cache_db))
148	    try:
149	        row = conn.execute(
150	            "SELECT b.request_object FROM cfurl_cache_blob_data b "
151	            "JOIN cfurl_cache_response r ON r.entry_ID = b.entry_ID "
152	            "WHERE r.request_key = ?",
153	            (SYNC_URL,),
154	        ).fetchone()
155	    finally:
156	        conn.close()
157	    if row is None:
158	        raise RuntimeError(
159	            "No cached /sync request in Cache.db. Open the Timelines app "
160	            "once to let it hit /sync, then retry."
161	        )
162	    archive = plistlib.loads(row[0])
163	
164	    token: str | None = None
165	    device_uuid: str | None = None
166	    for obj in archive.get("Array", []):
167	        if isinstance(obj, dict) and "Authorization" in obj:
168	            token = obj["Authorization"].removeprefix("Bearer ").strip()
169	        elif isinstance(obj, list) and obj and isinstance(obj[0], (bytes, bytearray)):
170	            try:
171	                raw = b"".join(obj)
172	                if raw[:2] == b"\x1f\x8b":
173	                    raw = gzip.decompress(raw)
174	                body = json.loads(raw.decode())
175	                device_uuid = body.get("deviceIdentifier")
176	            except Exception:
177	                continue
178	    if not token or not device_uuid:
179	        raise RuntimeError(
180	            "Could not extract token/device UUID from cached /sync request."
181	        )
182	    return SyncAuth(token=token, device_uuid=device_uuid)
183	
184	
185	# ---------- Hash formulas ----------
186	
187	
188	def hash_empty(counter: int) -> str:
189	    return hashlib.sha256(f"{counter}{SALT}".encode()).hexdigest()
190	
191	
192	def hash_write(changeset_id: str, counter: int) -> str:
193	    return hashlib.sha256(f"{changeset_id}{counter}{SALT}".encode()).hexdigest()
194	
195	
196	# ---------- Client ----------
197	
198	
199	class SyncClient:
200	    def __init__(self, auth: SyncAuth | None = None):
201	        self.auth = auth or load_auth()
202	
203	    def _post(self, body: dict, hash_: str) -> dict:
204	        data = json.dumps(body).encode()
205	        req = Request(
206	            SYNC_URL,
207	            data=data,
208	            method="POST",
209	            headers={
210	                "Content-Type": "application/json",
211	                "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.auth.token}",
212	                "X-Timelines-sync-hash": hash_,
213	            },
214	        )
215	        try:
216	            with urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
217	                return json.loads(resp.read().decode())
218	        except HTTPError as e:
219	            raise RuntimeError(
220	                f"/sync returned HTTP {e.code}: {e.read().decode(errors='replace')[:200]}"
221	            ) from e
222	        except URLError as e:
223	            raise RuntimeError(f"/sync network error: {e.reason}") from e
224	
225	    def discover_counter(self) -> int:
226	        """Empty-pull a far-future counter so the server replies with its own."""
227	        resp = self._post(
228	            {
229	                "deviceIdentifier": self.auth.device_uuid,
230	                "latestKnownGlobalCounter": DISCOVER_COUNTER,
231	                "changesets": [],
232	            },
233	            hash_empty(DISCOVER_COUNTER),
234	        )
235	        return int(resp["newGlobalCounter"])
236	
237	    def push_changeset(self, changeset: dict, counter: int) -> dict:
238	        """Send one changeset directly. Returns the server response dict."""
239	        body = {
240	            "deviceIdentifier": self.auth.device_uuid,
241	            "latestKnownGlobalCounter": counter,
242	            "changesets": [changeset],
243	        }
244	        return self._post(body, hash_write(changeset["id"], counter))
245	
246	    def pull_changesets_since(
247	        self, counter: int = 0, *, max_rounds: int = 200
248	    ) -> list[dict]:
249	        """Download every changeset with globalCounter > `counter`.
250	
251	        Paginated: each empty-pull returns at most `newChangesetsChangesCount`
252	        changes. We advance the counter based on the last changeset we saw and
253	        re-pull until `hasMoreChanges` is false. The server sometimes returns
254	        `newChangesets=[]` with `hasMoreChanges=true` during gaps; we still
255	        advance using `newGlobalCounter` so we never loop forever.
256	        """
257	        out: list[dict] = []
258	        seen_ids: set[str] = set()
259	        for _ in range(max_rounds):
260	            resp = self._post(
261	                {
262	                    "deviceIdentifier": self.auth.device_uuid,
263	                    "latestKnownGlobalCounter": counter,
264	                    "changesets": [],
265	                },
266	                hash_empty(counter),
267	            )
268	            ncs = resp.get("newChangesets") or []
269	            for cs in ncs:
270	                cs_id = cs.get("id")
271	                if cs_id and cs_id in seen_ids:
272	                    continue
273	                if cs_id:
274	                    seen_ids.add(cs_id)
275	                out.append(cs)
276	                gc = cs.get("globalCounter")
277	                if isinstance(gc, int) and gc > counter:
278	                    counter = gc
279	            server_counter = int(resp.get("newGlobalCounter") or counter)
280	            if server_counter > counter:
281	                counter = server_counter
282	            if not resp.get("hasMoreChanges"):
283	                return out
284	        raise RuntimeError(
285	            f"cloud pull exceeded {max_rounds} rounds — server kept reporting "
286	            "hasMoreChanges=true (possible pagination bug)"
287	        )
288	
289	
290	# ---------- Flushing pending ZCHANGESETs ----------
291	
292	
293	_ISO = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%f"
294	
295	
296	def _ms_to_iso(ms: float) -> str:
297	    """Convert unix-ms (Swift JSONEncoder.millisecondsSince1970) to ISO."""
298	    dt = datetime.fromtimestamp(ms / 1000, tz=timezone.utc)
299	    return dt.strftime(_ISO)[:-3] + "Z"  # trim to millisecond precision
300	
301	
302	def _convert_date_fields(obj: Any) -> Any:
303	    """Recursively convert numeric date fields in change dicts to ISO strings.
304	
305	    Swift's server expects ISO8601 strings for date fields; our ZCHANGESET
306	    blobs store them as floats (millisecondsSince1970). We fix them on the
307	    way out so the wire format matches what the app would send.
308	    """
309	    DATE_KEYS = {
310	        "startDate", "endDate", "lastModifiedDate", "dayIdentifier",
311	        "creationDate", "creationTime", "timestamp",
312	    }
313	    if isinstance(obj, dict):
314	        out = {}
315	        for k, v in obj.items():
316	            if k in DATE_KEYS and isinstance(v, (int, float)) and k != "dayIdentifier":
317	                out[k] = _ms_to_iso(float(v))
318	            else:
319	                out[k] = _convert_date_fields(v)
320	        return out
321	    if isinstance(obj, list):
322	        return [_convert_date_fields(v) for v in obj]
323	    return obj
324	
325	
326	def _deserialize_zchangeset_blob(blob: bytes) -> list[dict]:
327	    """ZCHANGESET blob columns are UTF-8 JSON arrays."""
328	    if not blob or blob == b"[]":
329	        return []
330	    return json.loads(blob.decode("utf-8"))
331	
332	
333	@dataclass
334	class FlushResult:
335	    pushed: int
336	    failed: int
337	    remaining: int
338	    errors: list[str]
339	
340	
341	def flush_pending_changesets(
342	    store_path: Path = STORE_PATH,
343	    client: SyncClient | None = None,
344	) -> FlushResult:
345	    """Walk ZCHANGESET where ZSTATE=2, POST each to /sync, delete on success.
346	
347	    Uses the cracked write-hash formula. Runs serially (one changeset per
348	    request) so the counter can advance between writes based on the server's
349	    response.
350	    """
351	    client = client or SyncClient()
352	    current_counter = client.discover_counter()
353	
354	    conn = sqlite3.connect(str(store_path))
355	    try:
356	        rows = conn.execute(
357	            "SELECT Z_PK, ZID, ZGLOBALCOUNTER, ZTIMESTAMP, "
358	            "ZEVENTCHANGES, ZTIMELINECHANGES, ZCOLORCHANGES, "
359	            "ZPREFERENCECHANGES, ZGOALCHANGES, ZCUSTOMOBJECTCHANGES "
360	            "FROM ZCHANGESET WHERE ZSTATE = 2 ORDER BY Z_PK"
361	        ).fetchall()
362	
363	        pushed = 0
364	        errors: list[str] = []
365	        for (
366	            z_pk, z_id_bytes, zg_counter, ztimestamp,
367	            evc, tlc, cc, pc, gc, coc,
368	        ) in rows:
369	            cs_id = str(uuid.UUID(bytes=bytes(z_id_bytes))).upper()
370	            ts_dt = datetime.fromtimestamp(
371	                float(ztimestamp) + CORE_DATA_EPOCH, tz=timezone.utc
372	            )
373	            ts_iso = ts_dt.strftime(_ISO)[:-3] + "Z"
374	
375	            changeset = {
376	                "id": cs_id,
377	                "timestamp": ts_iso,
378	                "globalCounter": current_counter,
379	                "modelVersion": 1,
380	                "eventChanges": _convert_date_fields(_deserialize_zchangeset_blob(evc)),
381	                "timelineChanges": _convert_date_fields(_deserialize_zchangeset_blob(tlc)),
382	                "customColorChanges": _convert_date_fields(_deserialize_zchangeset_blob(cc)),
383	                "preferenceChanges": _convert_date_fields(_deserialize_zchangeset_blob(pc)),
384	                "goalChanges": _convert_date_fields(_deserialize_zchangeset_blob(gc)),
385	                "customObjectChanges": _convert_date_fields(_deserialize_zchangeset_blob(coc)),
386	            }
387	            try:
388	                resp = client.push_changeset(changeset, current_counter)
389	                if resp.get("serverRejectedIncomingChanges"):
390	                    errors.append(f"cs={cs_id[:8]} rejected by server")
391	                    continue
392	                current_counter = int(resp["newGlobalCounter"])
393	                conn.execute("DELETE FROM ZCHANGESET WHERE Z_PK = ?", (z_pk,))
394	                conn.commit()
395	                pushed += 1
396	            except Exception as e:
397	                errors.append(f"cs={cs_id[:8]} {e}")
398	                break  # stop on first failure to keep state consistent
399	
400	        remaining = conn.execute(
401	            "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ZCHANGESET WHERE ZSTATE = 2"
402	        ).fetchone()[0]
403	    finally:
404	        conn.close()
405	
406	    return FlushResult(
407	        pushed=pushed,
408	        failed=len(errors),
409	        remaining=int(remaining),
410	        errors=errors,
411	    )

1	"""Core Data access layer for the Timelines app store.
2	
3	The Timelines app persists its data as a Core Data SQLite store shared across
4	its App Group. We open that store with Apple's own Core Data APIs (via pyobjc),
5	which means we get correct Z_PK allocation, optimistic locking, persistent
6	history tracking, and M:N relationship handling for free.
7	
8	To push writes up to the server, the Timelines app's SyncManager fetches
9	pending rows from ZCHANGESET (state=2) on its sync timer. Normally that row is
10	created inside the app's own NSManagedObjectContextWillSave hook — we're a
11	different process, so we ALSO emit a ZCHANGESET row ourselves via raw SQLite
12	after each write. The blob columns are plain UTF-8 JSON (Swift JSONEncoder
13	output with .millisecondsSince1970 date strategy), reverse-engineered from a
14	live capture of a pending row.
15	
16	When the Timelines app is running, it observes persistentStoreRemoteDidChange
17	and picks up both the entity tables and ZCHANGESET, fires its next sync, and
18	our changes land on the server — no HTTP hash cracking needed.
19	"""

511	                    empty_blob,
512	                    empty_blob,
513	                    timeline_blob,
514	                ),
515	            )

524	    """Swift-ish JSONEncoder output. Compact, no spaces, forward slashes escaped."""
525	    if not items:
526	        return b"[]"
527	    # json.dumps without spaces and with \/ escape of forward slashes
528	    raw = json.dumps(items, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False)
529	    raw = raw.replace("/", "\\/")

533	def _current_global_counter(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> int:

541	    import plistlib

"""Core Data access layer for the Timelines app store.

The Timelines app persists its data as a Core Data SQLite store shared across
its App Group. We open that store with Apple's own Core Data APIs (via pyobjc),
which means we get correct Z_PK allocation, optimistic locking, persistent
history tracking, and M:N relationship handling for free.

To push writes up to the server, the Timelines app's SyncManager fetches
pending rows from ZCHANGESET (state=2) on its sync timer. Normally that row is
created inside the app's own NSManagedObjectContextWillSave hook — we're a
different process, so we ALSO emit a ZCHANGESET row ourselves via raw SQLite
after each write. The blob columns are plain UTF-8 JSON (Swift JSONEncoder
output with .millisecondsSince1970 date strategy), reverse-engineered from a
live capture of a pending row.

When the Timelines app is running, it observes persistentStoreRemoteDidChange
and picks up both the entity tables and ZCHANGESET, fires its next sync, and
our changes land on the server — no HTTP hash cracking needed.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import sqlite3
import uuid
from contextlib import contextmanager
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Iterator

from Foundation import NSURL, NSDate, NSUUID  # type: ignore
from CoreData import (  # type: ignore
    NSEntityDescription,
    NSFetchRequest,
    NSManagedObject,
    NSManagedObjectContext,
    NSManagedObjectModel,
    NSMainQueueConcurrencyType,
    NSPersistentStoreCoordinator,
    NSSQLiteStoreType,
)


DEFAULT_MOMD = Path(
    "/Applications/Timelines.app/Wrapper/Timelines.app/Frameworks/"
    "PersistenceKit.framework/TimelinesModel.momd"
)
DEFAULT_STORE = Path.home() / "Library/Group Containers/group.glimsoft.Timelines/TimelinesModel"

# Persistent history + remote change notifications — both required for the
# running Timelines app to observe our writes via `persistentStoreRemoteDidChange:`.
NSPersistentHistoryTrackingKey = "NSPersistentHistoryTrackingKey"
NSPersistentStoreRemoteChangeNotificationPostOptionKey = (
    "NSPersistentStoreRemoteChangeNotificationPostOptionKey"
)
NSReadOnlyPersistentStoreOption = "NSReadOnlyPersistentStoreOption"

# changeType enum values observed in live sync traffic.
CHANGE_UPSERT = 100
CHANGE_PARTIAL = 200
CHANGE_DELETE = 300

# Z_ENT value for Changeset entity in this store's Z_PRIMARYKEY table.
CHANGESET_Z_ENT = 1

# ZSTATE value that the app's sync path treats as "pending outgoing push".
CHANGESET_STATE_PENDING = 2


# ---------- Date conversion ----------


def nsdate_to_datetime(d: Any) -> datetime | None:
    if d is None:
        return None
    return datetime.fromtimestamp(d.timeIntervalSince1970(), tz=timezone.utc)


def datetime_to_nsdate(dt: datetime) -> Any:
    if dt.tzinfo is None:
        dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    return NSDate.dateWithTimeIntervalSince1970_(dt.timestamp())


def datetime_to_unix_ms(dt: datetime) -> float:
    """Swift JSONEncoder .millisecondsSince1970 format used in ZCHANGESET blobs."""
    if dt.tzinfo is None:
        dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    return dt.timestamp() * 1000


# ---------- Store ----------


class TimelinesStore:
    """Wraps NSManagedObjectContext against the Timelines Core Data store.

    Usage:
        with TimelinesStore.open(readonly=False) as store:
            tl = store.find_timeline(name="Work")
            event = store.create_event(timeline=tl, name="Lunch",
                                       start=..., end=...)
            # save + emit ZCHANGESET happens automatically on context exit
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        ctx: Any,
        model: Any,
        psc: Any,
        store_path: Path,
        readonly: bool,
    ):
        self.ctx = ctx
        self.model = model
        self.psc = psc
        self.store_path = store_path
        self.readonly = readonly
        # Pending change records to emit as a ZCHANGESET on save.
        self._pending_event_changes: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
        self._pending_timeline_changes: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

    @classmethod
    def open(
        cls,
        *,
        readonly: bool = True,
        momd_path: Path = DEFAULT_MOMD,
        store_path: Path = DEFAULT_STORE,
    ) -> "TimelinesStore":
        if not momd_path.exists():
            raise FileNotFoundError(
                f"Timelines model bundle not found at {momd_path}. "
                "Is the Timelines app installed?"
            )
        if not store_path.exists():
            raise FileNotFoundError(
                f"Timelines store not found at {store_path}. "
                "Run the Timelines app once so it creates the store."
            )

        mom_url = NSURL.fileURLWithPath_(str(momd_path))
        model = NSManagedObjectModel.alloc().initWithContentsOfURL_(mom_url)
        if model is None:
            raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to load managed object model at {momd_path}")

        psc = NSPersistentStoreCoordinator.alloc().initWithManagedObjectModel_(model)
        store_url = NSURL.fileURLWithPath_(str(store_path))
        options: dict[str, Any] = {
            NSPersistentHistoryTrackingKey: True,
            NSPersistentStoreRemoteChangeNotificationPostOptionKey: True,
        }
        if readonly:
            options[NSReadOnlyPersistentStoreOption] = True

        store, err = psc.addPersistentStoreWithType_configuration_URL_options_error_(
            NSSQLiteStoreType, None, store_url, options, None
        )
        if store is None:
            raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to open persistent store: {err}")

        ctx = NSManagedObjectContext.alloc().initWithConcurrencyType_(
            NSMainQueueConcurrencyType
        )
        ctx.setPersistentStoreCoordinator_(psc)

        return cls(
            ctx=ctx, model=model, psc=psc, store_path=store_path, readonly=readonly
        )

    def __enter__(self) -> "TimelinesStore":
        return self

    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb) -> None:
        if exc_type is None and not self.readonly:
            if self.ctx.hasChanges() or self._pending_event_changes or self._pending_timeline_changes:
                self.save()

    # ---------- Fetch ----------

    def _fetch(self, entity_name: str, predicate: Any = None) -> list[Any]:
        fr = NSFetchRequest.alloc().initWithEntityName_(entity_name)
        if predicate is not None:
            fr.setPredicate_(predicate)
        results, err = self.ctx.executeFetchRequest_error_(fr, None)
        if results is None:
            raise RuntimeError(f"fetch({entity_name}) failed: {err}")
        return list(results)

    def fetch_events(self) -> list[Any]:
        return self._fetch("Event")

    def fetch_timelines(self) -> list[Any]:
        return self._fetch("Timeline")

    def fetch_custom_objects(self) -> list[Any]:
        return self._fetch("CustomObject")

    def fetch_preferences(self) -> list[Any]:
        return self._fetch("Preference")

    def find_timeline(
        self, *, name: str | None = None, uid: str | None = None
    ) -> Any | None:
        for t in self.fetch_timelines():
            if uid and t.uniqueIdentifier() == uid:
                return t
            if name and t.name() == name and not bool(t.archived()):
                return t
        return None

    def find_event(self, uid_prefix: str) -> Any | None:
        """Find an event by exact UUID or by a unique prefix."""
        matches = [
            e
            for e in self.fetch_events()
            if e.uniqueIdentifier().startswith(uid_prefix.upper())
        ]
        if len(matches) == 1:
            return matches[0]
        if len(matches) > 1:
            raise LookupError(
                f"ambiguous event prefix {uid_prefix!r}: {len(matches)} matches"
            )
        return None

    def running_timer(self) -> Any | None:
        for e in self.fetch_events():
            if bool(e.timeTrackingActive()):
                return e
        return None

    # ---------- Create / update / delete ----------

    def _require_write(self) -> None:
        if self.readonly:
            raise RuntimeError("store opened read-only")

    def _new_object(self, entity_name: str) -> Any:
        desc = NSEntityDescription.entityForName_inManagedObjectContext_(
            entity_name, self.ctx
        )
        return NSManagedObject.alloc().initWithEntity_insertIntoManagedObjectContext_(
            desc, self.ctx
        )

    def create_timeline(
        self,
        name: str,
        *,
        color: int = 0,
        order: int = 0,
        notes: str = "",
        daily_goal: int = 0,
        weekly_goal: int = 0,
        monthly_goal: int = 0,
        goals_are_limits: bool = False,
        unique_identifier: str | None = None,
    ) -> Any:
        self._require_write()
        t = self._new_object("Timeline")
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        now_ns = datetime_to_nsdate(now)
        uid = unique_identifier or str(NSUUID.UUID().UUIDString())
        t.setValue_forKey_(uid, "uniqueIdentifier")
        t.setValue_forKey_(name, "name")
        t.setValue_forKey_(notes, "notes")
        t.setValue_forKey_(color, "color")
        t.setValue_forKey_(order, "order")
        t.setValue_forKey_(False, "archived")
        t.setValue_forKey_(True, "visible")
        t.setValue_forKey_(daily_goal, "dailyGoal")
        t.setValue_forKey_(weekly_goal, "weeklyGoal")
        t.setValue_forKey_(monthly_goal, "monthlyGoal")
        t.setValue_forKey_(goals_are_limits, "goalsAreLimits")
        t.setValue_forKey_(now_ns, "creationTime")
        t.setValue_forKey_(now_ns, "lastModifiedDate")
        self._pending_timeline_changes.append(
            {
                "changeType": CHANGE_UPSERT,
                "uniqueIdentifier": uid,
                "name": name,
                "notes": notes,
                "color": color,
                "order": order,
                "archived": False,
                "visible": True,
                "dailyGoal": daily_goal,
                "weeklyGoal": weekly_goal,
                "monthlyGoal": monthly_goal,
                "goalsAreLimits": goals_are_limits,
                "creationTime": datetime_to_unix_ms(now),
                "lastModifiedDate": datetime_to_unix_ms(now),
            }
        )
        return t

    def create_event(
        self,
        *,
        timeline: Any,
        name: str = "",
        notes: str = "",
        start: datetime,
        end: datetime,
        marker_color: int | None = None,
        marker_size: int = 1,
        time_tracking_active: bool = False,
        was_time_tracked: bool = True,
        time_zone: str = "UTC",
        unique_identifier: str | None = None,
    ) -> Any:
        self._require_write()
        e = self._new_object("Event")
        uid = unique_identifier or str(NSUUID.UUID().UUIDString())
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        if marker_color is None:
            marker_color = int(timeline.color()) if hasattr(timeline, "color") else 0
        timeline_uid = str(timeline.uniqueIdentifier())
        day_id = start.astimezone().strftime("%Y-%m-%d")

        e.setValue_forKey_(uid, "uniqueIdentifier")
        e.setValue_forKey_(name, "name")
        e.setValue_forKey_(notes, "notes")
        e.setValue_forKey_(datetime_to_nsdate(start), "startDate")
        e.setValue_forKey_(datetime_to_nsdate(end), "endDate")
        e.setValue_forKey_(datetime_to_nsdate(now), "lastModifiedDate")
        e.setValue_forKey_(day_id, "dayIdentifier")
        e.setValue_forKey_(time_zone, "timeZone")
        e.setValue_forKey_(marker_color, "markerColor")
        e.setValue_forKey_(marker_size, "markerSize")
        e.setValue_forKey_(time_tracking_active, "timeTrackingActive")
        e.setValue_forKey_(was_time_tracked, "wasTimeTracked")
        timelines_set = e.mutableSetValueForKey_("timelines")
        timelines_set.addObject_(timeline)

        self._pending_event_changes.append(
            {
                "changeType": CHANGE_UPSERT,
                "uniqueIdentifier": uid,
                "name": name,
                "notes": notes,
                "startDate": datetime_to_unix_ms(start),
                "endDate": datetime_to_unix_ms(end),
                "lastModifiedDate": datetime_to_unix_ms(now),
                "dayIdentifier": day_id,
                "timeZone": time_zone,
                "markerColor": marker_color,
                "markerSize": marker_size,
                "timeTrackingActive": time_tracking_active,
                "wasTimeTracked": was_time_tracked,
                "timelineIdentifier": timeline_uid,
            }
        )
        return e

    def start_timer(
        self,
        *,
        timeline: Any,
        name: str = "",
        notes: str = "",
        time_zone: str = "UTC",
        at: datetime | None = None,
    ) -> Any:
        when = at or datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)
        return self.create_event(
            timeline=timeline,
            name=name,
            notes=notes,
            start=when,
            end=when,
            time_tracking_active=True,
            was_time_tracked=True,
            time_zone=time_zone,
        )

    def stop_timer(self, event: Any, *, at: datetime | None = None) -> None:
        self._require_write()
        when = at or datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        event.setValue_forKey_(datetime_to_nsdate(when), "endDate")
        event.setValue_forKey_(datetime_to_nsdate(now), "lastModifiedDate")
        event.setValue_forKey_(False, "timeTrackingActive")
        self._pending_event_changes.append(
            {
                "changeType": CHANGE_PARTIAL,
                "uniqueIdentifier": str(event.uniqueIdentifier()),
                "endDate": datetime_to_unix_ms(when),
                "timeTrackingActive": False,
                "lastModifiedDate": datetime_to_unix_ms(now),
            }
        )

    def update_event(self, event: Any, **fields: Any) -> None:
        self._require_write()
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        uid = str(event.uniqueIdentifier())
        change: dict[str, Any] = {
            "changeType": CHANGE_PARTIAL,
            "uniqueIdentifier": uid,
            "lastModifiedDate": datetime_to_unix_ms(now),
        }
        key_map: dict[str, tuple[str, Any]] = {
            "name": ("name", None),
            "notes": ("notes", None),
            "start": ("startDate", datetime_to_unix_ms),
            "end": ("endDate", datetime_to_unix_ms),
            "start_date": ("startDate", datetime_to_unix_ms),
            "end_date": ("endDate", datetime_to_unix_ms),
            "marker_color": ("markerColor", None),
            "marker_size": ("markerSize", None),
            "time_zone": ("timeZone", None),
            "day_identifier": ("dayIdentifier", None),
            "time_tracking_active": ("timeTrackingActive", None),
        }
        for key, value in fields.items():
            if key not in key_map:
                raise KeyError(f"unknown event field: {key}")
            wire_key, conv = key_map[key]
            if key in ("start", "end", "start_date", "end_date"):
                event.setValue_forKey_(datetime_to_nsdate(value), wire_key)
            else:
                event.setValue_forKey_(value, wire_key)
            change[wire_key] = conv(value) if conv else value
        event.setValue_forKey_(datetime_to_nsdate(now), "lastModifiedDate")
        self._pending_event_changes.append(change)

    def delete_event(self, event: Any) -> None:
        self._require_write()
        uid = str(event.uniqueIdentifier())
        self.ctx.deleteObject_(event)
        self._pending_event_changes.append(
            {
                "changeType": CHANGE_DELETE,
                "uniqueIdentifier": uid,
            }
        )

    def delete_timeline(self, timeline: Any) -> None:
        self._require_write()
        uid = str(timeline.uniqueIdentifier())
        self.ctx.deleteObject_(timeline)
        self._pending_timeline_changes.append(
            {
                "changeType": CHANGE_DELETE,
                "uniqueIdentifier": uid,
            }
        )

    # ---------- Save ----------

    def save(self) -> None:
        self._require_write()
        ok, err = self.ctx.save_(None)
        if not ok:
            raise RuntimeError(f"save failed: {err}")
        if self._pending_event_changes or self._pending_timeline_changes:
            self._emit_pending_changeset()

    def _emit_pending_changeset(self) -> None:
        """Insert a ZCHANGESET row so the app's SyncManager pushes our writes.

        The SyncManager reads rows where state=2 (pending) and sends them up
        on the next sync tick, then deletes them. We build the blobs with the
        same Swift JSONEncoder output (.millisecondsSince1970 date strategy)
        that the app uses internally.
        """
        # Use a separate SQLite connection for the Changeset insert so we don't
        # interfere with Core Data's transaction state.
        conn = sqlite3.connect(str(self.store_path))
        try:
            conn.execute("BEGIN IMMEDIATE")
            # Allocate next Z_PK for Changeset (Z_ENT = 1 per Z_PRIMARYKEY)
            cur = conn.execute(
                "SELECT Z_MAX FROM Z_PRIMARYKEY WHERE Z_ENT = ?",
                (CHANGESET_Z_ENT,),
            )
            row = cur.fetchone()
            if row is None:
                raise RuntimeError("Changeset entity not found in Z_PRIMARYKEY")
            z_max = row[0] + 1
            conn.execute(
                "UPDATE Z_PRIMARYKEY SET Z_MAX = ? WHERE Z_ENT = ?",
                (z_max, CHANGESET_Z_ENT),
            )

            event_blob = _json_blob(self._pending_event_changes)
            timeline_blob = _json_blob(self._pending_timeline_changes)
            empty_blob = b"[]"
            cs_uuid = uuid.uuid4().bytes
            # CoreData stores NSDate as a double = seconds since reference date (2001-01-01)
            now_mac = (datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()) - 978307200.0

            conn.execute(
                """INSERT INTO ZCHANGESET
                   (Z_PK, Z_ENT, Z_OPT, ZGLOBALCOUNTER, ZSTATE, ZTIMESTAMP, ZID,
                    ZCOLORCHANGES, ZCUSTOMOBJECTCHANGES, ZEVENTCHANGES,
                    ZGOALCHANGES, ZPREFERENCECHANGES, ZTIMELINECHANGES)
                   VALUES (?, ?, 1, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
                (
                    z_max,
                    CHANGESET_Z_ENT,
                    _current_global_counter(conn),
                    CHANGESET_STATE_PENDING,
                    now_mac,
                    cs_uuid,
                    empty_blob,
                    empty_blob,
                    event_blob,
                    empty_blob,
                    empty_blob,
                    timeline_blob,
                ),
            )
            conn.commit()
        finally:
            conn.close()
        self._pending_event_changes.clear()
        self._pending_timeline_changes.clear()


def _json_blob(items: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> bytes:
    """Swift-ish JSONEncoder output. Compact, no spaces, forward slashes escaped."""
    if not items:
        return b"[]"
    # json.dumps without spaces and with \/ escape of forward slashes
    raw = json.dumps(items, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False)
    raw = raw.replace("/", "\\/")
    return raw.encode("utf-8")


def _current_global_counter(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> int:
    cur = conn.execute(
        "SELECT ZVALUE FROM ZPREFERENCE WHERE ZNAME = 'kDefaultsLastKnownGlobalCounterKey'"
    )
    row = cur.fetchone()
    if row is None or row[0] is None:
        return 0
    # NSKeyedArchiver-encoded NSNumber — parse the plist.
    import plistlib

    try:
        p = plistlib.loads(row[0])
        for obj in p.get("$objects", []):
            if isinstance(obj, int) and obj >= 0:
                return obj
    except Exception:
        pass
    return 0


@contextmanager
def open_store(readonly: bool = True) -> Iterator[TimelinesStore]:
    store = TimelinesStore.open(readonly=readonly)
    try:
        yield store
    finally:
        pass

1	"""Cloud-state machinery for Timelines.app.
2	
3	Generic CRUD surface on top of ``api.SyncClient`` plus a persistent cache of
4	the last-seen ``/sync`` state so we don't re-download every changeset on each
5	invocation. Knows about ``Event`` / ``Timeline`` / ``CustomObject`` records and
6	the cloud changeset wire format.
7	
8	Countdown timers live as ``CustomObject`` records with
9	``objectType == "countdown-timer"`` (``stringValue1`` = timeline uid,
10	``stringValue2`` = name, ``stringValue3`` = duration seconds, ``stringValue4`` =
11	"true" while running). Starting one is two writes in a single changeset: a
12	running time-tracking ``Event`` plus a running countdown instance whose
13	``creationDate`` is the start time — the app then ticks it down and rings.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import json
19	import os
20	import sys
21	import time
22	import uuid as _uuid
23	from dataclasses import dataclass
24	from datetime import datetime, timezone
25	from pathlib import Path
26	from typing import Any
27	
28	from . import api
29	from .coredata import CHANGE_DELETE, CHANGE_PARTIAL, CHANGE_UPSERT, TimelinesStore
30	from .models import CustomObject, Event, Timeline
31	
32	COUNTDOWN_OBJECT_TYPE = "countdown-timer"
33	
34	
35	EPOCH = datetime.fromtimestamp(0, tz=timezone.utc)
36	
37	
38	# ---------- time helpers (generic) ----------
39	
40	
41	def local_naive(dt: datetime) -> datetime:
42	    if dt.tzinfo is not None:
43	        return dt.astimezone().replace(tzinfo=None)
44	    return dt
45	
46	
47	def local_aware(dt: datetime) -> datetime:
48	    if dt.tzinfo is not None:
49	        return dt
50	    return dt.astimezone()
51	
52	
53	def local_tz_name() -> str:
54	    try:
55	        return time.tzname[0] or "UTC"
56	    except Exception:
57	        return "UTC"
58	
59	
60	def floor_min(dt: datetime) -> datetime:
61	    """Truncate to whole minutes. Useful when LWW-comparing timestamps that
62	    come from different clocks (e.g. file mtime vs. Core Data) so sub-minute
63	    jitter doesn't count as a real edit.
64	    """
65	    return dt.replace(second=0, microsecond=0)
66	
67	
68	# ---------- wire format helpers ----------
69	
70	
71	def fmt_iso_ms(dt: datetime) -> str:
72	    if dt.tzinfo is None:
73	        dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
74	    return dt.astimezone(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%f")[:-3] + "Z"
75	
76	
77	def parse_iso(s: str | None) -> datetime | None:
78	    """Cloud returns ``2026-04-13T20:28:12.132Z``. Python <3.11 chokes on ``Z``."""
79	    if not s:
80	        return None
81	    if s.endswith("Z"):
82	        s = s[:-1] + "+00:00"
83	    try:
84	        return datetime.fromisoformat(s)
85	    except ValueError:
86	        return None
87	
88	
89	def now_iso() -> str:
90	    return fmt_iso_ms(datetime.now(timezone.utc))
91	
92	
93	def empty_changeset(counter: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
94	    return {
95	        "id": str(_uuid.uuid4()).upper(),
96	        "timestamp": fmt_iso_ms(datetime.now(timezone.utc)),
97	        "globalCounter": counter,
98	        "modelVersion": 1,
99	        "eventChanges": [],
100	        "timelineChanges": [],
101	        "customColorChanges": [],
102	        "preferenceChanges": [],
103	        "goalChanges": [],
104	        "customObjectChanges": [],
105	    }
106	
107	
108	def create_event_wire(
109	    uid: str,
110	    timeline_uid: str,
111	    *,
112	    name: str,
113	    notes: str,
114	    start: datetime,
115	    end: datetime,
116	    time_tracking_active: bool,
117	    marker_color: int,
118	) -> dict[str, Any]:
119	    """Build a full (changeType=100) upsert wire dict for an event.
120	
121	    ``marker_color`` matters: the Timelines app uses the event's markerColor
122	    to render its bar, not the timeline's color directly. Almost all events
123	    in the wild have markerColor == timeline.color, so callers pass that.
124	    """
125	    start = local_aware(start)
126	    end = local_aware(end)
127	    now = now_iso()
128	    return {
129	        "changeType": CHANGE_UPSERT,
130	        "uniqueIdentifier": uid,
131	        "timelineIdentifier": timeline_uid,
132	        "name": name or "",
133	        "notes": notes or "",
134	        "startDate": fmt_iso_ms(start),
135	        "endDate": fmt_iso_ms(end),
136	        "lastModifiedDate": now,

322	def apply_changesets(
323	    changesets: list[dict[str, Any]],
324	    events: dict[str, Event],
325	    timelines: dict[str, Timeline],
326	    custom_objects: dict[str, CustomObject] | None = None,
327	) -> None:
328	    """Fold changesets into existing event + timeline state in-place.
329	
330	    Changesets come in globalCounter order from the server; within one
331	    changeset we preserve list order, which matches the app's own writes.
332	    A partial change (200) against an unknown uuid is treated as an upsert
333	    on best-effort basis — in practice this happens when we receive a 200
334	    for an event that was created before our cache cursor.
335	    """
336	    changesets = sorted(changesets, key=lambda cs: cs.get("globalCounter") or 0)
337	
338	    for cs in changesets:
339	        for ec in cs.get("eventChanges") or []:
340	            uid = ec.get("uniqueIdentifier")
341	            if not uid:
342	                continue
343	            ct = ec.get("changeType")
344	            if ct == CHANGE_UPSERT:
345	                events[uid] = event_from_upsert(ec)
346	            elif ct == CHANGE_PARTIAL:
347	                if uid in events:
348	                    apply_partial(events[uid], ec)
349	                else:
350	                    events[uid] = event_from_upsert(ec)
351	            elif ct == CHANGE_DELETE:
352	                events.pop(uid, None)
353	
354	        for tc in cs.get("timelineChanges") or []:
355	            uid = tc.get("uniqueIdentifier")
356	            if not uid:
357	                continue
358	            ct = tc.get("changeType")
359	            if ct == CHANGE_UPSERT:
360	                timelines[uid] = timeline_from_upsert(tc)
361	            elif ct == CHANGE_PARTIAL:
362	                if uid in timelines:
363	                    apply_timeline_partial(timelines[uid], tc)
364	                else:
365	                    timelines[uid] = timeline_from_upsert(tc)
366	            elif ct == CHANGE_DELETE:
367	                timelines.pop(uid, None)
368	
369	        if custom_objects is None:
370	            continue
371	        for co in cs.get("customObjectChanges") or []:
372	            uid = co.get("uniqueIdentifier")
373	            if not uid:
374	                continue
375	            ct = co.get("changeType")
376	            if ct == CHANGE_UPSERT:
377	                custom_objects[uid] = custom_object_from_upsert(co)
378	            elif ct == CHANGE_PARTIAL:
379	                if uid in custom_objects:
380	                    apply_custom_object_partial(custom_objects[uid], co)
381	                else:
382	                    custom_objects[uid] = custom_object_from_upsert(co)
383	            elif ct == CHANGE_DELETE:
384	                custom_objects.pop(uid, None)
385

466	
467	def _load_cache(
468	    device_uuid: str,
469	) -> tuple[int, dict[str, Event], dict[str, Timeline], dict[str, CustomObject]]:
470	    """Return (counter, events, timelines, custom_objects). On any load failure
471	    — missing file, parse error, device-UUID mismatch — return (0, {}, {}, {})
472	    so the next pull is a full download.
473	    """
474	    try:
475	        raw = json.loads(CLOUD_CACHE_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
476	    except (FileNotFoundError, json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
477	        return 0, {}, {}, {}
478	
479	    if raw.get("device_uuid") != device_uuid:
480	        # Different device identity — cache is meaningless, start fresh.
481	        return 0, {}, {}, {}
482	
483	    if "customObjects" not in raw:
484	        # Old-format cache (pre custom-object support). Force one full re-pull so
485	        # custom objects created before the cursor get folded in.
486	        return 0, {}, {}, {}
487	
488	    events: dict[str, Event] = {}
489	    for uid, d in (raw.get("events") or {}).items():
490	        try:
491	            events[uid] = event_from_upsert(d)
492	        except Exception:
493	            return 0, {}, {}, {}
494	
495	    timelines: dict[str, Timeline] = {}
496	    for uid, d in (raw.get("timelines") or {}).items():
497	        try:
498	            timelines[uid] = timeline_from_upsert(d)
499	        except Exception:
500	            return 0, {}, {}, {}
501	
502	    custom_objects: dict[str, CustomObject] = {}
503	    for uid, d in (raw.get("customObjects") or {}).items():
504	        try:
505	            custom_objects[uid] = custom_object_from_upsert(d)
506	        except Exception:
507	            return 0, {}, {}, {}
508	
509	    return int(raw.get("counter") or 0), events, timelines, custom_objects
510	
511	
512	def save_cloud_state(
513	    counter: int,
514	    device_uuid: str,
515	    events: dict[str, Event],
516	    timelines: dict[str, Timeline],
517	    custom_objects: dict[str, CustomObject] | None = None,
518	) -> None:
519	    payload = {
520	        "counter": counter,
521	        "device_uuid": device_uuid,
522	        "events": {uid: _event_to_cache_dict(e) for uid, e in events.items()},
523	        "timelines": {uid: _timeline_to_cache_dict(t) for uid, t in timelines.items()},
524	        "customObjects": {
525	            uid: _custom_object_to_cache_dict(o)
526	            for uid, o in (custom_objects or {}).items()
527	        },
528	    }
529	    try:
530	        CLOUD_CACHE_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
531	        tmp = CLOUD_CACHE_PATH.with_suffix(".json.tmp")
532	        tmp.write_text(json.dumps(payload), encoding="utf-8")
533	        tmp.replace(CLOUD_CACHE_PATH)
534	    except OSError as ex:
535	        print(f"warn: failed to write cloud cache: {ex}", file=sys.stderr)
536	
537	
538	def fetch_cloud_state() -> tuple[
539	    dict[str, Event], dict[str, Timeline], dict[str, CustomObject], api.SyncClient, int
540	]:
541	    """Download cloud changesets since our last-seen counter and fold them
542	    into the cached state.
543	
544	    Returns the mutable ``(events_by_uid, timelines_by_uid, custom_objects_by_uid)``
545	    dicts plus the authenticated client and the current globalCounter, so callers
546	    that want to POST follow-up changesets don't have to re-auth or re-fetch. The
547	    cache is persisted with the pulled-through counter; callers that push
548	    must call :func:`save_cloud_state` again with the post-push counter.
549	    """
550	    client = api.SyncClient()
551	    counter, events, timelines, custom_objects = _load_cache(client.auth.device_uuid)
552	    changesets = client.pull_changesets_since(counter)
553	
554	    if changesets:
555	        apply_changesets(changesets, events, timelines, custom_objects)
556	        counter = max(
557	            (int(cs.get("globalCounter") or 0) for cs in changesets),
558	            default=counter,
559	        )
560	        save_cloud_state(counter, client.auth.device_uuid, events, timelines, custom_objects)
561	    elif counter == 0:
562	        # First-ever run with no changesets returned — empty cloud. Persist
563	        # so we don't retry from 0 on subsequent invocations.
564	        counter = client.discover_counter()
565	        save_cloud_state(counter, client.auth.device_uuid, events, timelines, custom_objects)
566	
567	    enrich_timeline_names(events, timelines)
568	    return events, timelines, custom_objects, client, counter
569

571	# ---------- direct cloud writes (high-level helper) ----------
572	
573	
574	class PushEventsResult:
575	    def __init__(self) -> None:
576	        self.requested: int = 0
577	        self.created: int = 0
578	        self.skipped_existing: int = 0
579	        self.unknown_timelines: list[str] = []
580	        self.created_uids: list[str] = []
581	        self.dry_run: bool = False
582	        self.error: str | None = None
583	
584	
585	def push_events_to_cloud(
586	    entries: list[dict[str, Any]],
587	    *,
588	    dry_run: bool = False,
589	) -> PushEventsResult:
590	    """Push a batch of closed-interval events to the Timelines cloud.
591	
592	    Each entry is a dict with keys:
593	      - ``timeline``: timeline NAME (resolved against current cloud state)
594	      - ``name``: event title (optional, defaults to "")
595	      - ``notes``: event notes (optional)
596	      - ``start``: ``datetime`` (timezone-aware preferred; naive treated as local)
597	      - ``end``: ``datetime``
598	
599	    Dedup is by ``(timeline_uid, name, start_minute)`` against current cloud
600	    state — entries that already exist are skipped (idempotent).
601	
602	    Returns a :class:`PushEventsResult` with counts and the list of new UIDs.
603	    """
604	    result = PushEventsResult()
605	    result.requested = len(entries)
606	    result.dry_run = dry_run
607	    if not entries:
608	        return result
609	
610	    try:
611	        events, timelines, _custom_objects, client, counter = fetch_cloud_state()
612	    except Exception as ex:
613	        result.error = f"cloud fetch failed: {ex}"
614	        return result
615	
616	    by_name: dict[str, Timeline] = {t.name: t for t in timelines.values() if not t.archived}
617	
618	    existing_keys: set[tuple[str, str, str]] = set()
619	    for e in events.values():
620	        if e.timeline_identifier and e.start_date:
621	            existing_keys.add((
622	                e.timeline_identifier,
623	                (e.name or "").strip(),
624	                fmt_iso_ms(local_aware(e.start_date))[:16],
625	            ))
626	
627	    wires: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
628	    new_uids: list[str] = []
629	    unknown: set[str] = set()
630	    for entry in entries:
631	        tl_name = entry["timeline"]
632	        tl = by_name.get(tl_name)
633	        if not tl:
634	            unknown.add(tl_name)
635	            continue
636	        name = (entry.get("name") or "").strip()
637	        start = entry["start"]
638	        end = entry["end"]
639	        key = (tl.unique_identifier, name, fmt_iso_ms(local_aware(start))[:16])
640	        if key in existing_keys:
641	            result.skipped_existing += 1
642	            continue
643	        existing_keys.add(key)
644	        uid = str(_uuid.uuid4()).upper()
645	        wire = create_event_wire(
646	            uid,
647	            tl.unique_identifier,
648	            name=name,
649	            notes=entry.get("notes") or "",
650	            start=start,
651	            end=end,
652	            time_tracking_active=False,
653	            marker_color=tl.color,
654	        )
655	        wires.append(wire)
656	        new_uids.append(uid)
657	
658	    result.unknown_timelines = sorted(unknown)
659	    if dry_run or not wires:
660	        result.created = 0 if dry_run else 0
661	        return result
662	
663	    cs = empty_changeset(counter)
664	    cs["eventChanges"] = wires
665	    try:
666	        resp = client.push_changeset(cs, counter)
667	        if resp.get("serverRejectedIncomingChanges"):
668	            result.error = "server rejected create changeset"
669	            return result
670	    except Exception as ex:
671	        result.error = f"push failed: {ex}"
672	        return result
673	
674	    result.created = len(wires)
675	    result.created_uids = new_uids
676	    return result
677

684	    change applied (best-effort, for display). ``stopped`` is a timer that was
685	    stopped as a side effect of ``start``. ``wires`` are the changeset event
686	    wires (shown on --dry-run)."""

690	        self.dry_run: bool = False
691	        self.error: str | None = None

697	def find_running(events: dict[str, Event]) -> Event | None:
698	    """The time-tracking-active event, if any (latest-started wins on ties)."""
699	    running = [e for e in events.values() if e.time_tracking_active]
700	    if not running:
701	        return None
702	    running.sort(
703	        key=lambda e: local_aware(e.start_date)
704	        if e.start_date
705	        else EPOCH
706	    )
707	    return running[-1]

710	def resolve_timeline(timelines: dict[str, Timeline], ref: str) -> Timeline | None:
711	    """Resolve a timeline by exact name (case-insensitive), then unique UUID prefix."""
712	    ref = ref.strip()
713	    for t in timelines.values():

1037	def _to_countdown(
1038	    o: CustomObject, timelines: dict[str, Timeline], now: datetime
1039	) -> Countdown:
1040	    tl = timelines.get(o.string_value_1)
1041	    running = (o.string_value_4 or "").lower() == "true"
1042	    duration = _int_or_zero(o.string_value_3)

1051	        timeline_name=tl.name if tl else "",
1052	        timeline_color=tl.color if tl else 0,
1053	        duration_seconds=duration,
1054	        running=running,
1055	        started=local_aware(o.creation_date) if running and o.creation_date else None,
1056	        remaining_seconds=remaining,

1234	    name: str | None = None, *, at: datetime | None = None, dry_run: bool = False

1242	    except Exception as ex:

1244	        return r
1245	    targets = _running_countdowns(custom_objects)
1246	    if name:

1248	    if not targets:

1250	        return r

aiTitle: Check timelines sync with cloud changes

content.text:
Timelines sync is healthy, and the "wrapper around the atomic changes" you're describing already exists in the repo. Here's what I found.

## Sync status: OK

`tt doctor` and `tt status` both pass against `cloud.timelines.app`:
- Auth resolves from `TIMELINES_AUTH_TOKEN` + `TIMELINES_DEVICE_UUID` (device `F1FE4349…`), cloud reachable, current `globalCounter` **7705**.
- 13 active timelines, 4014 events, one timer currently running (**Transportation, unnamed, since 11:10** — ~3h, worth a glance in case it's a forgotten timer, but that's data not a sync fault).
- The atomic change feed decodes cleanly — I pulled `changes --since 7700` and every op reconstructs into full event state (create carries all fields, edit carries only the changed subset, delete carries none), exactly as designed.

## The atomic-change model, and the wrapper that already wraps it

You're right that the cloud speaks in atomic changesets — each carries per-event ops: **upsert=100 (create)**, **partial=200 (edit, changed fields only)**, **delete=300**, ordered by `globalCounter`. That lives in [coredata.py](../Timelines-Sync/timetrack/coredata.py) (the enum + Core Data write path) and the wire format in [sync.py](../Timelines-Sync/timetrack/sync.py).

The read-only wrapper you're suggesting is already built — [timetrack/changes.py](../Timelines-Sync/timetrack/changes.py), surfaced as `tt changes`:
- `tt changes` → current counter (a seed point to start watching from "now")
- `tt changes --since N [--json]` → every event change newer than counter N, decoded

Its module docstring is explicit that it's **"stateless and side-effect-free: it talks to the cloud and returns data"** — pure reads, no cursor persisted, no pushes. That's the safe inspection surface.

## About "redoing changes breaks the cloud" — that risk is on the *write* path, and it's already guarded

The danger you remember is **replaying/pushing** changesets, not reading them. That path is [api.py](../Timelines-Sync/timetrack/api.py) `push_changeset` / `flush_pending_changesets` and the `_commit*` helpers in sync.py. What makes a push "illegal" to the server is the sync-hash + counter binding: `sha256(changeset_id + counter + SALT)`, and the counter must be current. Push with a stale counter or re-push an already-applied changeset and the server returns `serverRejectedIncomingChanges` (or the counter desyncs). The existing code already defends against exactly that:
- `discover_counter()` before each push, and advances the counter from each server response;
- pushes **serially, one changeset per request** (`flush_pending_changesets`), and **stops on the first failure** to keep state consistent;
- checks `serverRejectedIncomingChanges` on every response;
- `push_events_to_cloud` dedups by `(timeline_uid, name, start_minute)` so re-runs are idempotent.

## My recommendation

Nothing needs building for inspection — use `tt changes` / `tt status`; they're read-only and safe to run freely. I didn't touch the write path.

If your actual goal is something the current wrapper doesn't cover, tell me which and I'll build it safely (dry-run first):
- a **watch/tail** mode (`tt changes` on an interval, persisting a cursor) for a live feed;
- resolving `timelineIdentifier` → timeline name and formatting durations in the change output;
- or a specific **replay/redo** operation — which is the genuinely risky one, so I'd want to confirm the exact semantics before writing anything that pushes.

Which of those (if any) did you have in mind?
